prompt:
Here's our weekly campaign numbers for the Q1 ABM push — Week 4 of 8. Target is 35 SQLs by end of quarter (so ~4.4/week run rate). We're behind.

LinkedIn Sponsored:
  Impressions: 42,300
  Clicks: 11...
assertions: LinkedIn CTR (0.28%) is compared against the benchmark table (average: 0.35-0.45%) and classified as below average, not just reported as a number

Email sequence decay is root-cause diagnosed before any recommendation — the analysis distinguishes between subject line issues (open rate problem) and body content issues (open-to-click drop) to explain why Email 3 underperforms

Each recommendation is specific enough to execute immediately — e.g., not 'improve the landing page' but a concrete change like restructuring the form, reducing fields, or adding social proof with expected conversion lift
A 'What is working — do not change' section explicitly names Email 1 CTR (5.2%, good tier) and budget pacing as things to protect from unnecessary optimization
Webinar-to-opp rate (8%) is flagged as poor against the 15-20% average benchmark, with root cause analysis exploring whether the problem is attendee quality, follow-up timing, or post-webinar nurture
The action plan includes specific owners and deadlines for each item, not generic 'team should review' statements
